title: Causal stint change-point features instead of more fixed tyre-life constants
summary: RUL and recent F1 tyre-state papers both emphasize regime changes: the useful signal is often the onset of a new degradation phase, not tyre age alone.
rationale: RUL and recent F1 tyre-state papers both emphasize regime changes: the useful signal is often the onset of a new degradation phase, not tyre age alone. In pit strategy terms, the cliff matters more than the clock.
implementation_hint: Within each (Race_Year, Driver, Stint), compute only backward-looking features: rolling slope and acceleration of LapTime_Delta, residual pace vs same-race-lap peers, rolling variance jumps, run length of consecutive deterioration, and a piecewise-linear breakpoint score indicating the first sustained pace collapse.
expected_effect: Should sharpen predictions for imminent stops triggered by sudden degradation or traffic-induced tyre overheating that static life-to-finish rules miss.
risk: Raw lap times are noisy because of traffic, yellow-flag effects, and out/in laps; the residualization step has to stay causal and robust.
